value: MVA ED/hospital: high-speed, rollover/ejection, death in same vehicle, prolonged extrication, LOC/AMS, anticoagulants, pregnancy, neuro deficits, chest/abd pain, seatbelt sign.

C-spine imaging: midline tenderness, neuro deficit, intoxication, distracting injury, AMS. Canadian C-spine high-risk: age ≥65, dangerous mechanism, paresthesias. Low-risk + able rotate 45° may avoid imaging.
T/L spine imaging: midline back tenderness, neuro deficit, high-energy mechanism, older age/osteoporosis, anticoagulants, or concerning pain pattern.

Peds head CT (PECARN style): CT/obs if AMS, palpable skull fracture, basilar skull signs, severe mechanism, LOC, persistent vomiting, severe headache. Use age <2 vs ≥2 criteria; consider observation when intermediate risk.

Adult/elderly head CT: anticoagulants/antiplatelets, age ≥65, vomiting, severe/worsening headache, seizure, neuro deficit, basilar skull signs, dangerous mechanism, or persistent AMS. Lower threshold in elderly.
